PREGUNTAS:
1. How does the attendance policy handle cases of misuse or abuse of the attendance rules, and what potential consequences could Ciklumers face if found guilty of such misconduct?

2. What is the protocol for handling deviations from the standard work schedule, and who must approve these changes for both Billable Talent Teams and Non Billable Talent Teams?

3. Can Ciklumers working in Client teams expect the same rules as outlined in the attendance policy, and who is responsible for managing any specific rules for these teams?
---
Attendance Policy | Spain
Contents
Definitions
1. Preamble
2. Work Schedule
3. Public Holidays
4. Unavailability/Vacation/Leave
5. Incapacity/Sick Leave
6. Other Types of Leave
Definitions
Ciklumer is an Individual who has cooperation relations with Ciklum IT Spain SL.
Line Manager is a manager who is responsible for unavailability/vacation/leave days approval for Ciklumer for Non Billable Talent Teams and to 
whom Ciklumer should report.
Delivery Manager is a manager who is responsible for unavailability/vacation/leave days approval for Ciklumer for Billable Talent Teams.
HR Partner is a person responsible for support of  Delivery & Engineering Teams or Business Teams
1. Preamble
1.1 This policy sets out our arrangements for reporting and managing Ciklumers’ working schedule and periods of unavailability due to different 
reasons for handling employee absences and tardiness to promote the efficient operation and minimize unscheduled absences. 
1.2 Unavailability / vacation / leave and incapacity / sickness are to be reported and managed in accordance with this policy.  
1.3 Abuse of attendance rules, including failing to report unavailability / vacation / leave and incapacity / sickness or falsely claiming one will be treated 
as misconduct under Ciklum internal policies and may lead to disciplinary sanctions. 
1.4 The policy outlines a standard approach, Ciklumers in Client teams may have their own specific rules described in handbooks managed by 
assigned HR Partner. 
1.5 This Policy may be amended by the Company at any time.
2. Work Schedule
2.1 Unless otherwise regulated by local legislation, policies and Ciklumer’s job / cooperation offer:  
2.1.1 Usual working hours start 9:00-10:00, end 18:00-19:00 local time Monday to Friday, provided that 40 hours weekly shall be worked out.  
2.1.2. Lunch break is not included into employee’s working time and is not paid. 
2.2.3 As a rule, working beyond normal working schedule, as stated above, is not allowed. Any deviations must be agreed with the Line Manager/ 
Delivery Manager (for Billable talents).
3. Public Holidays
3.1. During each holiday year, Ciklum Spain follows a determined public holidays schedule. Bank holidays are considered as days off:
New Year’s Day (1 Jan)
Epiphany (6 Jan)
 day (28 Feb)
Andalusia
 (28 March)
Maundy Thursday
 (29 March)
Good Friday
 (1 May)
Labour Day
 (15 Aug)
Assumption of Mary
 (19 Aug)
Malaga's Saint Patrons Day
 (9 Sep)
Malaga's Our Lady of Victory Day
 (12 Oct)
Hispanic Heritage Day
 (1st Nov)
All Saints' Day
 (6th Dec)
Constitution Day

PREGUNTAS:
1. If a Ciklumer has been with the company since January of the current year and utilized only 2 of their 4 permitted undocumented sick leave days by the end of the calendar year, what happens to the remaining 2 unused days?

2. How should a Billable Talent in Ciklum Digital proceed if they need to request an emergency unavailability/vacation/leave day and the Line Manager is not available to approve it immediately?

3. Can a Ciklumer who started on March 15th request the full 23 working days of paid vacation within their first year, and are there any conditions or recommendations regarding the utilization of these days?
---
(9th Dec)
Immaculate Conception
Christmas Day (25 Dec)
3.2. The exact Public Holidays dates for each Calendar Year will be set in line with governmental announcements and will be reflected on SESAME in 
advance.
4. Unavailability/Vacation/Leave
4.1 Unavailability/Vacation/Annual Leave is a short-term period of time during each year when Ciklumer is allowed to cease fulfilling duties / providing 
services for the Company to have rest. 
4.2 Unless otherwise regulated by local legislation, policies and Ciklumer’s job / cooperation offer:  
4.2.1 Ciklumer is granted with 23 working days of paid unavailability /vacation/leave annually accumulated pro rata starting from the first day of 
cooperation.  
4.2.2 Line Manager/ Delivery Manager (for Billable talents) is responsible for appropriate unavailability/vacation /leave days scheduling and 
unavailability/vacation /leave days utilization of Ciklumers in his unit.
4.2.3 Unavailability/vacation /leave days could be splitted into parts. 
4.2.4 Ciklum recommends that all holiday entitlement is used during the current year. Untaken holiday entitlement can be carried forward until 31 
March next year only. Failure to do so, the remaining holiday entitlement will be lost 
4.3. Ciklum required Ciklumers to follow recommended terms of unavailability/vacation/leave days request submission:
for Non-Billable Talents in Ciklum:
4.3.1 Ciklum recommends that all vacation days should be requested by a Ciklumer and approved by the Line Manager on SESAME at least 2 weeks 
in advance.
for Billable Talent in Delivery & Service Lines (Ciklum Digital):
4.3.2 Ciklum recommends that unavailability/vacation/leave days should be requested in advance based on unavailability duration:
Up to 2 working days should be requested 3 business days in advance
Up to 10 working days inclusive should be requested 1 month in advance 
Beyond 10 working days should be requested 2 months in advance
4.3.3 In emergency situation, unavailability/vacation/leave day can be requested day to day or 1 day in advance
4.3.4 All vacation days should be requested by a Ciklumer and approved by the Delivery Manager/ Line Manager and Local HR on SESAME
5. Incapacity/Sick Leave
5.1 Incapacity/Sick Leave is the period of time when Ciklumer cannot fulfill duties / provide services for the Company due to illness or injury. 
5.2 In case of incapacity/sick days, Ciklumer should inform his / her Line Manager/ Delivery Manager (for Billable talents) and update SESAME as 
soon as possible.  
5.3 Unless otherwise regulated by local legislation, policies and Ciklumer’s job / cooperation offer:  
5.3.1 Up to 4 incapacity days in 12-month period from the cooperation start may be undocumented, i.e. do not require a medical certificate (parte de 
baja medico):
In case Ciklumer started with the Company current year, then he/she is granted up to 4 working days proportionally to the number of days 
since start day in the team / client / department till the end of current calendar year. For example, if Ciklumer started on July, 1st, he will have 
2 rounded total undocumented days allowed by the end of this calendar year;
All days are provided cumulatively on a start date in the team / client / department;
The number of days per year does not increase if a person changes the team / client / department, but stays 4 days per year granted pro-rata;
In case Ciklumer has started previous year, then he/she is allowed to request full 4 days within current year. However, no more than 3 days 
in a row are allowed; 
5.3.2 All accrued, but unused days of undocumented sick / incapacity days are to be cut on Jan, 1st each year. Current year undocumented sick / 
incapacity days are provided first day of the year in full.  
5.3.3 In case Ciklumer starts in a new team within 3 months after his / her exit date with another team / client / department, undocumented sick / 
incapacity days will be granted pro-rata on start date, but with respect of the limit of no more than 4 days in a 12-month period. 
5.4 All incapacity/ sick days must be recorded by Ciklumer on 
. Without l Social Security certification (baja medica)) these days should be 
SESAME
registered as an undocumented sick leave 
6. Other Types of Leave

PREGUNTAS:
1. ¿Qué procedimientos debe seguir un empleado de Ciklum Spain si ha agotado todos sus días de vacaciones pagadas y necesita tomar días de ausencia sin goce de sueldo por razones personales o familiares?

2. Si un empleado cree que podría tener derecho a un tipo de permiso legal no mencionado en la Política de Asistencia de Ciklum, ¿qué paso debe tomar para explorar su elegibilidad para dicho permiso según las leyes españolas?

3. ¿Cuáles son los nombres de las personas que revisaron y aprobaron la versión 1.1 de la Política de Asistencia de Ciklum para España y cuándo fue aprobada oficialmente esta versión?
---
6.1 In exceptional cases, when Ciklumer used all paid vacation days, (s)he may take unpaid absence days (e.g., due to personal / family reasons) 
upon agreeing with the Line Manager/ Delivery Manager (for Billable talents).
6.2. This Policy addresses those leaves of absence that the Company has found are the most commonly requested by its Employees. There may be, 
however, additional types of leaves available under the Spanish law.
6.3. If you believe that you may be entitled to a statutory leave of absence that is not set forth in this Policy, please contact your HR Partner to discuss 
your potential eligibility for one of these leaves.
